title: euonymus fortunei f. radicans
intro: Euonymus fortunei f. radicans, also known as the climbing euonymus or wintercreeper, is a woody, evergreen climber native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing cholesterol levels, lowering blood pressure, and exhibiting anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Euonymus fortunei f. radicans can be used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
